page: Microsoft Azure E32ads_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Eadsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor operating at 4.5 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16, and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU.

This server includes 1890 GB of local SSD storage and supports Premium Storage. In benchmark testing, the E32ads_v7 demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core capabilities, as evidenced by its stress-ng and PassMark CPU scores. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks, such as gzip and bzip3, also rank in the top tier. Memory bandwidth and database operations perform in the average tier, while static web serving throughput is strong.

While the system excels in CPU-bound tasks and small-model LLM text generation, it exhibits poor performance for large-scale LLM inference, such as text generation with a 70B parameter model, due to the lack of dedicated GPU hardware. The high memory density and local SSD storage make this instance highly cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads without the overhead of GPU accelerators. It is best suited for relational databases, in-memory caching systems, data analytics, and memory-heavy enterprise applications.
description: The Microsoft Azure E32ads_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by a 32-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Featuring 256.0 GB of RAM and 1890 GB of local SSD storage, this x86_64 instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads. Benchmark results show top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core capabilities, alongside top-tier single-threaded compression speeds. However, memory bandwidth and database operations remain in the average tier, and the lack of a GPU results in poor performance for large-scale LLM inference. The E32ads_v7 offers a balanced, high-density memory profile with local storage, making it a cost-efficient choice for relational databases, in-memory caches, and enterprise applications requiring substantial memory capacity without GPU acceleration.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32ads_v7 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 32 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor, 256 GB of RAM, and 1890 GB of local SSD storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32ads_v7 is a memory-optimized VM with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1890 GB local SSD, built for databases and memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high-frequency AMD EPYC processing, dense RAM, and fast local SSD storage for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor at 4.5 GHz
256.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU
1890 GB of local SSD storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Well-suited for relational databases, in-memory caching, and data analytics
